title: arthropodium dyeri
intro: Arthropodium dyeri, also known as the New Zealand flax lily or milk parsley, is a flowering plant native to New Zealand. The plant's rhizomes contain a compound called flavonoid, which is believed to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Arthropodium dyeri include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's rhizomes are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
